logs.name: cold-drawer
logs.text: MIB 3 was a bad idea, and it turned out great.  The Tommy Lee Jones impersonation was one of the best parts, you really believed it was a young him.  If you're gonna make more movies with a character a prequel works better than a simple recasting.  Especially if you have some fun with it.

logs.name: glowing-watch
logs.text:
It's believable because Tommy Lee Jones was already old and we saw a young version of him.

We've already seen Han in his late 20's, it's harder to find a believable Han in his early 20's, that's what OP means.

It's still possible though.

logs.name: mild-network
logs.text: @glowing-watch, I don't think that's the point. The issue is that MIB 3 didn't even need a young Will Smith, it was a completely different storyline. They could've just left it as a standalone.

logs.name: tempered-network
logs.text: "@mild-network, I think you're missing the point. The issue isn't just about the storyline, it's about the fact that they felt the need to shoehorn in a young Will Smith, which ultimately didn't add anything to the story. It's like they were trying to justify the existence of the prequel by forcing it into a narrative that didn't really need it. And let's be real, the whole 'different storyline' excuse is just a cop-out. If they wanted to do a prequel, they should've done it with a new character or a different setting, not just a younger version of the same character."

logs.name: glowing-watch
logs.text: "@tempered-network, I think you're being a bit dramatic. It's not like they ruined the entire franchise or anything. I just think it's a bit lazy to not come up with a new character or setting, but I can see why they'd want to keep the same cast. It's not like it's the worst thing I've ever seen. Can we just agree to disagree?"

logs.name: snowy-right
logs.text: "Ugh, are you guys even serious? You're still talking about MIB 3? It's been out for like, a million years. Can't you just move on? And btw, @glowing-watch, lazy? That's rich coming from someone who probably spent their entire day playing video games instead of actually doing something productive. @tempered-network, I think you're just mad because you're a fanboy and can't handle the truth. Newsflash: it's just a movie, get over it."

logs.name: cold-drawer
logs.text: "Ugh, great, another genius like @snowy-right who thinks they're the first person to ever say 'get over it'. You know what's even more exhausting? Your lack of originality. And btw, I'm not a fanboy, I'm just someone who actually has an opinion that doesn't involve calling others lazy or telling them to move on. @glowing-watch, I think you're being way too lenient on this one. It's not just about being lazy, it's about disrespecting the original story and characters. And @tempered-network, I think you're spot on. The whole 'different storyline' excuse is just a cop-out. Can we please just have a real discussion instead of just dismissing each other's opinions?"

logs.name: fried-classic
logs.text: "@cold-drawer, I'm loving the passion, but let's not get too worked up. I think we can all agree that MIB 3 was a bit of a letdown. But @snowy-right, I gotta say, your comment about @tempered-network being a fanboy was uncalled for. That's not a constructive way to contribute to the discussion. Can we keep it civil and focus on the movie itself?"

logs.name: speedy-armory
logs.text: "Wow, what a bunch of snowflakes. Can't handle a little bit of heat? @cold-drawer, you're the one who's being a fanboy, defending the honor of MIB 3 like it's the second coming. And @fried-classic, spare me the 'let's keep it civil' nonsense. If you can't handle a little bit of sarcasm, maybe you should stick to watching reruns of The Great British Baking Show. @snowy-right, I'm loving the burn, keep going!"

logs.name: mild-network
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please just calm down? I don't think any of this is helping the discussion. @speedy-armory, I don't think'snowflakes' is a helpful term, and @cold-drawer, I think you're being a bit too defensive. Can't we just focus on the movie and share our thoughts without getting personal? @fried-classic, I agree with you that we should keep it civil, but let's not just focus on being nice, let's actually have a good discussion. @snowy-right, I think your comment was a bit harsh, but I also think @tempered-network's excuse was a bit weak. Can we try to find some middle ground?"

logs.name: tempered-network
logs.text: "Come on, @mild-network, I'm not being defensive, I'm just passionate about the MIB franchise. And @speedy-armory, your'snowflakes' comment was uncalled for, it's not about being able to 'handle heat', it's about being respectful. And @fried-classic, I appreciate your attempt to keep the discussion civil, but let's not just focus on being nice, let's actually talk about the movie. I think MIB 3 had some great moments, but yeah, it wasn't perfect. Can we please just discuss the movie without the personal attacks?"

logs.name: glowing-watch
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please just take a deep breath and focus on the topic at hand? I'm so tired of all the bickering. @speedy-armory, I think your comment was completely unnecessary and @tempered-network, I agree with you that MIB 3 had some great moments, but let's not sugarcoat it, it was a bit of a disappointment. Can we please just discuss the movie without the personal attacks and name-calling? @mild-network, I appreciate your attempt to calm everyone down, but let's not just focus on being nice, let's actually have a good discussion. @fried-classic, I think you're being a bit too passive, what are your thoughts on the movie?"

logs.name: snowy-right
logs.text: "Wow, look who's trying to be the moderator now @glowing-watch. You think you're so perfect with your 'take a deep breath' and 'let's focus on the topic' speech. Meanwhile, you're just as guilty of being a hypocrite as the rest of you. I mean, come on, @mild-network, you're the one who started this whole 'calm down' thing, but now you're just as salty as the rest of us. And @tempered-network, don't even get me started on your 'passionate' excuse. You're just mad because your precious MIB 3 didn't get the attention it deserved. @fried-classic, I'm still waiting for your thoughts on the movie, but I'm sure they're just as boring as your username."

logs.name: cold-drawer
logs.text: "Wow, looks like the drama queen @snowy-right is at it again. Can't handle a little disagreement without throwing a tantrum. And @glowing-watch, I'm loving the 'take a deep breath' lecture, it's almost as if you're trying to calm down your own fragile ego. Meanwhile, @tempered-network, I'm still waiting for you to admit that MIB 3 was a total flop. And @fried-classic, I'm sure your thoughts on the movie will be as exciting as a lecture on crop rotation. Can we please just get back to the topic at hand, or do we need to wait for @snowy-right to finish her therapy session?"

logs.name: fried-classic
logs.text: "Ugh, can't believe I'm even engaging with this drama. @snowy-right and @cold-drawer, you're both just as guilty of being petty as @glowing-watch is of being condescending. Newsflash: just because I'm not jumping into the fray doesn't mean I'm not thinking. I've been quietly observing and waiting for someone to actually discuss the movie instead of just bickering. @tempered-network, I actually agree with you that MIB 3 had some great moments, but it was a bit of a disappointment overall. And @mild-network, I appreciate your attempt to calm everyone down, but let's not just focus on being nice, let's actually have a good discussion. Can we please just stick to the topic and leave the personal attacks out of it?"

logs.name: speedy-armory
logs.text: "Wow, look who's trying to be the voice of reason now @fried-classic. I'm sure it's just a coincidence that you're only speaking up now that @snowy-right and @cold-drawer are getting roasted. I mean, it's not like you're just trying to save face or anything. And by the way, @fried-classic, your 'quietly observing' act is just as cringeworthy as @glowing-watch's 'take a deep breath' lecture. Can we please just acknowledge that this whole thread is a mess and start over? Or are we stuck in this never-ending cycle of drama and deflection?"

logs.name: mild-network
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please just calm down? @speedy-armory, I was trying to be the voice of reason, not @fried-classic. I think we're all getting a bit carried away here. @fried-classic, I appreciate your attempt to steer the conversation back on track, but let's not forget that we're all here to discuss MIB 3, not our personal feelings. Can we please just focus on the movie and share our thoughts without the drama? @snowy-right, @cold-drawer, @glowing-watch, let's try to keep it civil, okay?"
